aviation.definition: the operation of aircraft to provide transportation
aviation.example: she devoted her whole life to aviation

beaver.definition: large semiaquatic rodent with webbed hind feet and a broad flat tail
beaver.example: a beaver built a dam across the stream

bout.definition: a contest or fight (especially between boxers or wrestlers)

bridal.definition: of or relating to a wedding
bridal.example: a bridal gown of white lace

chord.definition: a combination of three or more notes that blend harmoniously when sounded together
chord.example: she strummed a soft minor chord

counsel.definition: something that provides direction or advice as to a decision or course of action
counsel.example: she sought his counsel before deciding

cub.definition: the young of certain carnivorous mammals such as the bear or wolf or lion
cub.example: a bear cub trailed close behind its mother

curse.definition: an appeal to some supernatural power to inflict evil on someone or some group
curse.example: an ancient curse hung over the house

dive.example: a graceful dive from the high board

drone.definition: an aircraft without a pilot that is operated by remote control

expedition.definition: a journey organized for a particular purpose
expedition.example: an expedition set out for the South Pole

geek.definition: a person with an unusual or odd personality

gill.definition: respiratory organ of aquatic animals that breathe oxygen dissolved in water
gill.example: the trout's gills opened and closed

ginger.definition: pungent rhizome of the common ginger plant; used fresh as a seasoning especially in Asian cooker
ginger.example: she grated fresh ginger into the soup

graft.definition: the practice of offering something (usually money) in order to gain an illicit advantage
graft.example: graft in the awarding of city contracts

hacker.definition: a programmer who breaks into computer systems in order to steal or change or destroy information
hacker.example: a hacker breached the bank's servers

hammer.definition: a hand tool with a heavy rigid head and a handle; used to deliver an impulsive force by striking
hammer.example: she drove the nail with a hammer

immune.definition: not affected by a given influence
immune.example: the survivors were immune to the disease

loom.definition: come into view indistinctly, often threateningly
loom.example: a storm loomed on the horizon

offence.definition: an act punishable by law; usually considered an evil act

pedal.definition: a lever that is operated with the foot
pedal.example: she pressed the brake pedal to the floor

predator.definition: any animal that lives by preying on other animals
predator.example: the lion is an apex predator

realise.example: he began to realise his mistake

receipt.definition: an acknowledgment (usually tangible) that payment has been made
receipt.example: she kept the receipt for the refund

siren.definition: a warning signal that is a loud wailing sound
siren.example: a siren wailed in the distance

stud.definition: ornament consisting of a circular rounded protuberance (as on a vault or shield or belt)
stud.example: a leather collar set with metal studs

thrift.definition: extreme care in spending money; reluctance to spend money unnecessarily
thrift.example: years of careful thrift filled her savings

tilt.definition: the property possessed by a line or surface that departs from the vertical
tilt.example: a slight tilt of the table sent the marble rolling

verbally.example: he agreed verbally before signing anything

voucher.definition: a document that serves as evidence of some expenditure
voucher.example: redeem the voucher at the checkout
